steps.title: Test whether the proxy matches
steps.content: The hypothesis says Ag-NP bioaccumulation in D. melanogaster could stand in for costly sampling of vertebrates such as X. laevis. To support that proxy role, the two species must show similar bioaccumulation behavior under comparable conditions.

steps.title: Reject nonmatching evidence
steps.content: Choice A compares tolerance, not uptake rates. Choice B says fruit-fly concentrations vary more, weakening reliability. Choice D compares detection difficulty rather than whether the species accumulate Ag-NPs similarly. The remaining finding shows comparable uptake in comparable environments. The answer is C.
